ground_truth.title: An introduction to social commerce: What brands need to know in 2026
ground_truth.main_content:
If you're not turning Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok into direct sales platforms with social commerce, you're leaving cold, hard cash on the table. Your customers are already there – and many of them want to shop. About half of users say they've made purchases directly from social media within the past 90 days.

Social commerce, otherwise known as social shopping or social media shopping, is the use of social media to sell products directly to consumers. By definition, social commerce is a type of eCommerce. However, there's a key difference between true social commerce and other types of eCommerce. In traditional eCommerce, retailers sell products on their own apps and websites. They may advertise items on social media, but shoppers have to click through to the retailer's online store to investigate and buy a product. In social commerce, retailers sell products on social media networks. Consumers can discover and purchase products entirely within the platform, mid-scroll.

The outlook is bright for social shopping. By the end of 2026, social commerce sales are expected to surpass $100 billion for the first time. Social commerce allows brands to sell to consumers where they are, make sales fast and seamless, and drive sales through personalization.

Many different types of consumer products can benefit from social commerce. Apparel and accessories lead the way. Beauty and personal care, home decor and furniture, food and beverage, and electronics are the next most successful categories. Social commerce works well for new and unique products, as well as those featuring aspirational imagery.

Common social commerce mistakes include using a one-size-fits-all approach across platforms, not tracking inventory across platforms, and ignoring comments or messages about the shopping experience. Customer service is essential to social commerce, with 58% of social media users saying it's important that brands respond to their customers and followers on social media.

The top channels for social commerce are Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and WhatsApp. Instagram is currently the social commerce leader in terms of revenue, estimated at $37.2 billion in 2024. TikTok Shop revenue surged 650% to $33.2 billion in the same year. TikTok has quickly emerged as a leader, with 11% of all U.S. households making a purchase on TikTok since the Shop was introduced.
with: Social commerce, otherwise known as social shopping or social media shopping, is the use of social media to sell products directly to consumers.
In social commerce, retailers sell products on social media networks. Consumers can discover and purchase products entirely within the platform, mid-scroll.
By the end of 2026, social commerce sales are expected to surpass $100 billion for the first time.
Instagram is currently the social commerce G.O.A.T. in terms of revenue, but TikTok is following quickly on its heels.
Fifty-eight percent of social media users say that it's important that brands they buy from respond to their customers/followers on social media.
